skill.name: azure-compute
skill.description: Recommend Azure VM sizes, VM Scale Sets, and pricing options from workload requirements using Microsoft documentation and the Azure Retail Prices API.
skill.summary: Helps choose Azure VM and VMSS options with current pricing and workload trade-offs.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, URL, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found the command and network hits are documentation examples for Microsoft Learn and the unauthenticated Azure Retail Prices API, while weak-crypto and reconnaissance hits are false positives from Markdown text and Azure terminology. No prompt injection, credential access, secret exfiltration, or malicious execution intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Public Network Requests for Current Azure Data
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to fetch Microsoft Learn pages and query prices.azure.com. This is legitimate for current Azure recommendations, but it creates a network dependency and should be limited to the documented Microsoft endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced URLs are public Microsoft documentation and Azure pricing endpoints. The risk is operational rather than malicious because no credential transfer or third-party exfiltration endpoint was found.

medium_findings.title: Shell Examples for Retail Prices API Queries
medium_findings.description: The reference guide includes curl and PowerShell examples for querying the unauthenticated Azure Retail Prices API. The examples use fixed public endpoints, but generated commands should still avoid untrusted shell interpolation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are examples for a known public API and do not include dynamic execution of local user input. The remaining concern is that assistants may adapt shell snippets with unsanitized user-provided filters.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static weak-crypto findings appear to match Markdown text, Azure SKU names, CPU vendor names, and documentation wording rather than cryptographic functions. No hashing, encryption, credential handling, or crypto API use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose, tables, and Azure naming text, not executable cryptographic code. No evidence found for weak algorithms such as MD5 or SHA1 being used for security decisions.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Benign Documentation Text
low_findings.description: Static reconnaissance findings map to Azure monitoring and pricing guidance, not local host enumeration. The skill does not instruct collection of local system, network, process, or environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content describes Azure autoscale diagnostics and API pagination. No evidence found of commands such as hostname, whoami, ipconfig, process listing, or local file discovery.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown examples include curl and Invoke-RestMethod commands. They are directed at public Azure pricing data and are not evidence of malware, but they are command patterns that require careful handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and easy to audit, and they target prices.azure.com only. Risk would increase if user input were inserted into shell commands without encoding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hardcoded Public URLs
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill and references contain hardcoded Microsoft Learn and Azure Retail Prices API URLs. These support the skill purpose and do not indicate exfiltration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are all public Microsoft or Azure endpoints needed for current recommendations. No unrelated webhook, paste site, or attacker-controlled domain was found.

content.user_title: Choose Azure VM and VMSS Options
content.value_statement: Azure VM selection is difficult because sizes, pricing, and scale set behavior change often. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through workload requirements, current documentation checks, and pricing comparisons.
actual_capabilities: Maps workload requirements to Azure VM families such as D, E, F, L, NC, ND, NV, DC, and HB series.
Helps decide between a single VM and a Virtual Machine Scale Set based on scaling and availability needs.
Guides current pricing lookups with the unauthenticated Azure Retail Prices API.
Compares pay-as-you-go, reservation, savings plan, Spot, Linux, and Windows pricing considerations.
Recommends VMSS orchestration, autoscale, and load balancing patterns for common workloads.
Provides trade-off explanations for cost, performance, availability, and operational complexity.
limitations: It does not deploy, modify, or inspect Azure resources in a tenant.
It depends on live Microsoft documentation and pricing data for final accuracy.
It provides planning guidance, not formal capacity testing or benchmark results.
It does not replace Azure Pricing Calculator for full environment estimates.

use_cases.title: Plan a production web tier
use_cases.description: Compare single VM and VMSS options, choose a VM family, and estimate hourly costs for expected traffic levels.

use_cases.title: Size a data workload
use_cases.description: Match database, cache, analytics, or storage-heavy workloads to memory or storage optimized Azure VM families.

use_cases.title: Estimate compute budget options
use_cases.description: Review pay-as-you-go, reservation, savings plan, and Spot pricing trade-offs before requesting Azure budget approval.

prompt_templates.title: Recommend a VM Size
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend an Azure VM size for a small Linux web application in eastus. Prioritize low cost and explain the trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Compare VM Families
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare D-series, B-series, and F-series VMs for a CI workload that runs during business hours. Include cost and performance trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a VMSS Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a VM Scale Set recommendation for a stateless API with variable traffic. Include orchestration mode, autoscale rules, load balancing, and estimated cost range.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Pricing Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare pay-as-you-go, reservation, savings plan, and Spot pricing for three candidate VM sizes in westus3. Explain when each option is appropriate.

output_examples.input: I need a low-cost VM for a small development database.
output_examples.output: The skill returns two or three Azure VM choices, explains why burstable or memory optimized options fit, and notes cost and performance trade-offs.

output_examples.input: Should I use VMSS for a public API?
output_examples.output: The skill compares a single VM with VMSS, recommends a scaling model when appropriate, and describes autoscale and load balancer choices.

output_examples.input: Estimate monthly compute cost for three candidate sizes.
output_examples.output: The skill summarizes pricing from the Azure Retail Prices API, converts hourly prices into monthly estimates, and highlights reservation or Spot options.

best_practices: Provide workload type, region, operating system, instance count, and budget priority before asking for recommendations.
Verify final SKU availability and specifications against current Microsoft documentation.
Ask for at least two alternatives so cost, performance, and availability trade-offs are visible.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to deploy VMs, change Azure resources, or inspect an existing subscription.
Do not rely on cached reference tables for final prices without checking live pricing data.
Do not choose VMSS only for high availability when one or two simple VMs would meet the requirement.

faq.question: Does this skill require an Azure account?
faq.answer: No. It uses public Microsoft documentation and the unauthenticated Azure Retail Prices API for planning guidance.

faq.question: Can it deploy a VM or scale set?
faq.answer: No. It recommends options only. Use a deployment-focused Azure skill or Azure tooling for implementation.

faq.question: Can it look up my existing Azure VMs?
faq.answer: No. It does not inspect subscriptions or resources. It works from requirements supplied in the prompt.

faq.question: How accurate are pricing estimates?
faq.answer: They can be current when live pricing is checked, but complete budgets should include storage, networking, licensing, and discounts.

faq.question: Does it support VM Scale Sets?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps decide when VMSS is appropriate and recommends orchestration, autoscale, and load balancing patterns.

faq.question: Can it recommend GPU or HPC VMs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for GPU, HPC, confidential, memory optimized, storage optimized, and general purpose families.
